text:
Angela Erdmann never met her grandfather. He died in 1946, six years before she was born.

On Tuesday 8th April, 2014, she told the story of an amazing event. She received a message in a bottle. Her grandfather had thrown it into the Baltic Sea 101 years earlier. People think it may be the world’s oldest message in a bottle. A museum in Germany gave it to her, and the museum is now showing it.

Erdmann, 62, said it was a big surprise. She said a man came to her door and told her he had mail from her grandfather. He explained that a message in a bottle had been found, and the name on the card was her grandfather’s. The man was a genealogy researcher. He found her in Berlin after the letter was given to the International Maritime Museum in Hamburg.

The bottle was a brown beer bottle. It had been in the water for 101 years. A fisherman named Konrad Fischer found it in his catch while fishing in the Baltic Sea near the city of Kiel. Holger von Neuhoff, a curator at the museum, said it was the oldest bottled message he had seen. He said the museum has older documents that were found without a bottle, but this bottle and its document are the oldest set like this. He said it was in very good condition.

Researchers believe Erdmann’s grandfather, Richard Platz, threw the bottle into the sea in 1913. He was hiking with a nature group at the time. He was 20 years old.

Most of the postcard was hard to read. But the Berlin address on the front could be read. The card also politely asked the person who found it to send it to his home address.

Erdmann said her grandfather also put two stamps from that time in the bottle, so the finder would not have to pay. But he did not think it would take 101 years.

Erdmann said she felt very touched by the message. She did not know her grandfather because he died at age 54, before she was born.

She said she knew little about him, but she learned that he was a writer. She said he was open-minded, believed in freedom, and believed people should respect each other. She said he helped young people and later travelled with his wife and two daughters. She said it was wonderful because it helped her see where her family came from.

Erdmann said she also likes culture and travelling around the world, like her grandfather. She said she is open-minded too. She said what he taught his two daughters, her mother taught her, and she has passed it on to her sons.

Even though she was happy to get the message, she said she hopes people today will not throw bottles with messages into the sea. She said the sea is already full of bottles and rubbish.

The message and the bottle will be on display at Hamburg’s Maritime Museum until the beginning of May 2014. After that, experts will try to read the rest of the text. It is not clear what will happen to the bottle after that, but Erdmann hopes it will stay at the museum.

She said they want to add a few photos with the bottle, so visitors can see the young man who threw it into the water.

text:
A thick layer of bird droppings covers a shiny railing at one of Britain’s most expensive homes. Dead pigeons lie near broken mirrors, and water runs through damaged parts of the ceiling. This house is called The Towers. It is a £30m palace on “Billionaires’ Row” in north London. Its terrible condition has been kept secret until now.

The Towers is one of ten mansions on The Bishops Avenue. This road is in the middle of London’s rising property market. These houses have been almost empty since they were bought about 25 years ago. People believe they were bought for members of the Saudi Arabian royal family. The houses are falling apart. Their Greek-style columns are breaking. Their swimming pools, covered with mosaic tiles, are full of rubble. Nature has taken over so much that owls now live there.

This sad sight happens again and again on this famous street. Lloyds Bank says it is the second most expensive street in Britain. At the same time, more people are struggling to buy a home in London because prices rise 11.2% each year. But 16 mansions on the most expensive part of The Bishops Avenue are empty. Many are behind locked gates. Their windows are covered with steel bars, and their gardens are overgrown. Guard dogs walk around the grounds.

Across the street is another empty mansion worth £18m. Its windows are smashed, and its walls have anti-climb paint. Another house has metal bars on the windows and was sold for £20m.

Even so, prices keep rising. Dryades, a mansion that was owned until recently by a Pakistani politician, sold for £12m in 2007 and is believed to be worth about £30m today. Heath Lodge is worth £13m today. It was the place where fashion tycoon Aristos Constantinou was murdered in 1984 with a silver bullet. Heath Lodge was sold in the late 1970s for £400,000.

Seeing these empty, broken houses can be very painful for people who struggle to afford housing in one of the world’s most expensive cities. A security guard on the street said it was upsetting to watch so much space—enough room for dozens of people—falling apart.

In the past, royals with oil money from Nigeria and Saudi Arabia came to this curved road near Hampstead Heath. Iranians came after the fall of the shah. Now, Chinese buyers are following Russians and Kazakhs. They spend millions to get an address that estate agents say is as famous as the Champs Elysées and Rodeo Drive. Recently, two mansions were for sale for £65m and £38m. They promised Italian marble, lifts with leather padding, and luxury panic rooms.

But on the grounds of the empty houses, stone fountains are breaking, and lawns have turned into muddy bogs. Inside one house, water drips through a huge crystal chandelier onto a thick carpet that is rotting under parts of a collapsed ceiling. Moss grows through broken bricks, and pieces of mirror tile are spread across a bathroom. The swimming pool has about a foot of dirty, salty water, and flowers grow through the tiles. Wooden boards in the sauna are bending out.

The worst damage is at The Towers. It is a large mansion surrounded by many trees, including hornbeams, oaks, and limes. Its big halls with high ceilings are full of pigeons. Water pours through three floors, and the walls have turned bright green with algae. The water falls into a huge, empty basement swimming pool. There are unopened wooden crates marked “bullet-proof glass,” showing the old owners had security worries.

Today, very few people live on The Bishops Avenue all the time. A security guard outside one mansion said the owners were not there. Another guard outside Royal Mansion would not say if anyone was home. A worker at another mansion warned the Guardian about guard dogs. Magdy Adib Ishak-Hannah, an Egyptian-born private healthcare businessman with personal wealth of £45m, said he is one of the few people who lives there permanently.

He said, “It’s not a friendly place where you can chat over the fence. To be honest, I have never seen what my neighbours look like. Next door, a Saudi princess spent £35m on a new house and I’ve never seen her. There are about three houses that are lived in 24/7 and half of the properties are occupied three to six months a year. The other half, who knows if they come or not?”

These broken, very expensive homes show a culture where the richest people in the world buy British property as investments. One Hyde Park, an apartment building in Knightsbridge, is another example. More than half the flats there are registered with the council as empty or second homes.

Still, people on the avenue talk about building £5m apartments instead of £50m mansions, to try to bring people back.

Anil Varma, a local property developer, plans to rebuild one valuable site on the avenue into 20 apartments. The building would have a concierge, maid service, a 25-metre pool, a spa, and a cinema.

He said, “If you build a big house and try and sell for £30m to £40m, it won’t sell. Locals won’t buy and so you have to bring in overseas buyers.”

But using these empty homes to help solve the housing crisis still seems far away. Andrew Harper, a local Conservative councillor, laughed when asked if some of the broken houses could become affordable homes. He said the land price would be too high.

He said, “Very wealthy people own property there. Sometimes they live in them and sometimes they don’t.”

This house is called The Towers. It is a £30m mansion on “Billionaires’ Row” in north London. People did not know how ruined it was until now.

The Towers is one of ten mansions on The Bishops Avenue. This street is a very expensive part of London. These houses have been almost empty for about 25 years. It is believed they were bought for members of the Saudi Arabian royal family. The columns are breaking, and swimming pools with mosaic tiles are full of broken pieces. Nature has taken over, and owls now live there.

This empty and ruined scene can be seen along the street. Lloyds Bank says The Bishops Avenue is the second most expensive street in Britain. At the same time, more people in London cannot afford a home because prices rise 11.2% each year. On the most expensive part of The Bishops Avenue, 16 mansions are empty. Many are behind locked gates, with metal covers on windows, overgrown gardens, and guard dogs.

Across the street is another empty mansion worth £18m, with broken windows and special paint to stop people climbing. Another mansion has metal bars on the windows and was sold for £20m.

Even so, prices keep rising. Dryades, a mansion that was owned until recently by a Pakistani politician, sold for £12m in 2007 and is believed to be worth about £30m today. Heath Lodge, where fashion tycoon Aristos Constantinou was murdered in 1984 with a silver bullet, is worth £13m today. It was sold in the late 1970s for £400,000.

This is painful for people who cannot afford housing in one of the world’s most expensive cities. A security guard said it was upsetting to see so much space—enough for dozens of people—falling apart.

Rich royal families from Nigeria and Saudi Arabia were some of the first to come to this road near Hampstead Heath. Iranians came here after the fall of the shah. Now, Chinese buyers are following Russians and Kazakhs. Estate agents say the address is as famous as the Champs Elysées and Rodeo Drive. Recently, two mansions were for sale for £65m and £38m. They promised Italian marble, lifts with leather inside, and panic rooms.

But outside these empty homes, stone fountains are breaking and lawns have turned into wet bogs. Inside one house, water drips through a big crystal chandelier onto a thick carpet. Parts of the ceiling have fallen down, and the carpet is rotting. Moss grows through broken bricks, and broken mirror tiles cover a bathroom. The swimming pool has about one foot of dirty salty water, and flowers grow through the tiles. Wooden slats in the sauna bend outward.

The most dramatic ruin is The Towers. It is a large mansion among many trees. Pigeons fill its tall rooms. Water pours through three floors, and algae has turned the walls bright green. The water falls into a huge, empty basement swimming pool. Unopened wooden boxes marked “bullet-proof glass” show the old owners were worried about security.

Today, very few people live on The Bishops Avenue all the time. One security guard said the owners of a mansion were not there. Another guard, outside Royal Mansion, would not say if anyone was home. A worker at another mansion warned the Guardian about the guard dogs. Magdy Adib Ishak-Hannah, an Egyptian-born private healthcare mogul worth £45m, said he is one of the few people who live there permanently.

He said it is not a friendly place where you talk to neighbours. He said he has never seen what his neighbours look like. He said a Saudi princess next door spent £35m on a new house, but he has never seen her. He said about three houses are lived in all day and night. About half the properties are used only three to six months a year. He said for the other half, nobody knows if the owners come or not.

These ruined mansions show a culture where very rich people buy British homes as investments. One Hyde Park, a group of apartments in Knightsbridge, is another example. More than half the flats there are registered with the council as empty or second homes.

Still, people on the avenue talk about building £5m apartments instead of £50m mansions, to bring people back.

Anil Varma, a local developer, plans to rebuild one valuable site as 20 apartments. They would have a concierge, maid service, a 25-metre pool, a spa, and a cinema.

He said if you build a big house and try to sell it for £30m to £40m, it will not sell. He said local people will not buy, so you must find buyers from overseas.

But using these empty homes to help solve the housing crisis still seems far away. Andrew Harper, a local Conservative councillor, laughed when asked if some of the ruined homes could become affordable housing. He said the land price would be too high.

He said very wealthy people own property there. Sometimes they live in the homes, and sometimes they do not.

text:
One beautiful summer evening, I decided to swim near Doolin Pier in County Clare, Ireland. I moved there in 2012. In the water, a woman was swimming with Dusty, a dolphin. Dusty often swims with a group of people.

Dusty came to Doolin around 2008. Since then, hundreds of people have swum with her, so she can seem very tame. She was even in an Irish tourist-board ad, where a girl says she wants to touch a dolphin.

That evening, the woman was tickling Dusty’s tummy. It looked friendly and safe. About 20 tourists and local people were watching from the pier. Soon after I got into the water, Dusty left the woman and suddenly went wild. Later I learned she is very territorial when she is with someone. Her tail was hitting the water hard. At first I thought she was just showing off, but then I realized she might be angry.

I knew I needed to get out, so I swam toward the pier. But in a split second, Dusty crashed into me with her nose. It was very strong and very painful. I was thrown forward.

People on the pier stared at me in shock. Dusty stayed next to me, still hitting the water with her tail. That scared me the most. I thought, if she hits me with her tail, I could sink.

text:
James Hamblin is a senior editor at The Atlantic magazine. In his series “If Our Bodies Could Talk,” he tried a challenge: he stopped showering for a while. He also took fewer showers and did not use shampoo or soap when he did shower. He did this to see what happens when we clean our bodies too much.

He learned what many others have found. When we use lots of soap, body wash, and scrubbers, our skin works harder to get back to normal. Showering can wash away the skin’s natural oils and helpful bacteria. Hot water and cleaning products can make this worse.

After a shower, you might feel like your skin is too tight. That can happen because your skin’s natural moisture gets washed away. Our skin also has millions of helpful bacteria, like the gut does. Frequent scrubbing can destroy many of these bacteria. When the bacteria come back, the new mix may include more microbes that cause smell. So showering too often may make you smell more. If you stop showering and stop using soap, your skin may go through an early adjustment time that can seem gross. After that, the skin often returns to balance, makes less oil, and healthy bacteria grow again.

After his experiment, Hamblin felt the human body can be nice when it works on its own. It is not only about smell or looks. Dermatologists say using less soap can help acne and eczema.

Taking fewer showers can also help the environment. The average shower lasts seven minutes and uses 65 litres of water. That is 65 litres of clean, drinkable water mixed with soap and sent down the drain every day, sometimes more than once.

Clean water is becoming harder to ignore, especially as California faces another summer of drought. It is clear that clean water is one of the world’s most valuable resources, and it may soon be in short supply. Also, body wash bottles affect the environment. These are strong reasons to let your body be more natural.

If this idea makes you feel worried, relax. Many people who cut back on washing still use deodorant if they need it. Everyone’s natural smell is different, and it can change with diet, how much water you drink, and exercise. Also, washing your hands with soap is still important to help stop infectious diseases from spreading.

You do not have to stop all at once like Hamblin did. Between cleaning many times a day and never showering, there is a middle choice. Try to reduce. Skip a few showers, use less soap, and let your skin’s helpful bacteria grow.

story_id: WNL are you a bad boyfriend
story_key: wnl are you a bad boyfriend
text:
Dan Sullivan was late to breakfast with the Guardian. He was late because police stopped him while he was riding his motorcycle.

Sullivan says he works too much. He misses dinner dates. He forgets to give presents.

So, like many people in Silicon Valley, the 27-year-old venture capitalist made a start-up to help: BetterBoyfriend.me. This service sends girlfriends and wives a present every month for about $70.

Sullivan works in finance. He has been testing the program quietly. He has signed up about 350 boyfriends. This brings in about $17,000 each month. He says most members are his Harvard and investor friends. They include venture capitalists, founders, and employees at companies like Apple, Google, and Facebook. Their girlfriends have been getting presents from Sullivan for the last year.

Each month, members choose from a list of seven gifts, like chocolate, tea sets, and manicures. The package is sent to the boyfriend with no brand name and no label.

Sullivan tests ideas by buying Facebook ads to see what results he gets. BetterBoyfriend.me became popular. Sullivan said, “BetterBoyfriend hit something. It’s a pain point everyone has.”

BetterBoyfriend.me was not his first idea. For a couple of days, he thought about sharing dogs. He said he saw rich guys looking miserable walking dogs. He said a dog is only valuable 5% of the time. He wondered if someone could babysit the dogs and other people could rent them.

Another start-up is called InvisibleBoyfriend. It lets users design a believable social partner who is everything you want and is always available to talk. The result is a “boyfriend” who can text with you all day.

The founder, Kyle Tabor, launched it to help users stop unwanted advances by using a fake boyfriend. But he found that people became attached to their computer-made partners. He said many more users want companionship through conversation, instead of needing outside “proof” of a relationship.

For Sullivan and Tabor, a surprise was the real relationships they formed with customers. Sullivan says he has started to see himself as a kind of relationship consultant for the boyfriends.

Sullivan admits he made mistakes. At first, the packages included receipts with his name, Dan Sullivan. One boyfriend wrote to him and said, “Listen, dude, she’s not mad but Cynthia found out.”

About 50% of the women who get packages know about Sullivan’s role. He says it connects to age. He thinks that after you have been married a while, you do not keep many secrets.

Over the year, Sullivan says he got to know the boyfriends very well. They even did a delivery to a hospital delivery room. He said the key is to remember that his relationship is with the boyfriend.

When he first started the company, he put BetterBoyfriend.me tags on flower bouquets. Then he went to Dolores Park, a popular place in the Mission District with many tech workers and young people.

He looked for couples and gave the flowers to the girl, but the boyfriends did not like it. He said it made sense. Then he changed his plan and started giving the flowers to the boyfriends.

text:
Opposition to Western Australia’s shark cull has grown. Thousands of people went to beaches around Australia to ask the state premier to stop the policy. RSPCA Australia and Virgin Atlantic owner Richard Branson also spoke against it.

The policy catches and kills sharks longer than three metres. It began after what the state government called an “unprecedented” number of shark attacks on Western Australia’s coast. A 35-year-old surfer was killed in November 2013. He was the sixth person to die from a shark attack in two years.

However, the Shark Attack File says Australia as a whole has averaged one shark-related death a year for the last 50 years.

Kate Faehrmann, a board member of the Sea Shepherd Conservation Society, spoke at a protest in Perth. She said the policy will not work. She said drumlines are not selective. She said they can kill sharks of any size, and also dolphins, turtles, and other animals. She said this is why the community does not want the policy.

Thousands of people protested at Perth’s Cottesloe Beach and Sydney’s Manly Beach. Hundreds also protested at Glenelg in south-west Adelaide, and at beaches in Victoria and Queensland. Faehrmann said the protests showed many Australians want sharks protected. She said people are coming out across the country to say the ocean belongs to sharks too and they do not want them killed.

Anthony Joyce, a surfer who once had his foot caught in a shark’s mouth, said the number of sharks killed would make no difference.

The state government has refused to give an ongoing count of sharks killed. Reports say sharks smaller than three metres have been released after being caught on drumlines. Drumlines are floating drums anchored to the sea bed, with bait on hooks under them.

Conservationists say there is no evidence the cull will reduce shark attacks on humans. They say no earlier cull used only drumlines. Researchers at the University of Western Australia say the recent increase in attacks may be linked to the state having the fastest-growing population in Australia, not to more sharks.

Richard Peirce, chairman of the UK charity the Shark Trust, said the cull would not work and could bring more predators near the coast. He said it is sad the government did not follow advice and did not try other options instead of killing animals. He also said that leaving drumlines in overnight could attract other predators, because sharks and other animals may be hooked and injured.

Around the world in 2012, there were 80 unprovoked shark attacks, and seven were fatal. In the same period, humans kill nearly 100 million sharks each year. RSPCA Australia said the cull is not justified. It said there is no evidence attacks are increasing because shark numbers are increasing. It said the increase fits with more people being in the water because of changes in population and human behaviour.

Richard Branson told Fairfax Radio the policy was backfiring. He said Premier Colin Barnett may have thought it would help tourism, but he believes it will do the opposite. He said it makes the problem seem bigger than it is and may stop people from coming to Perth and nearby countryside. He said it will only worry people for no good reason.
